method: For each of 10 sliding 5-Myr LA2004 windows, computed Welch PSD and measured the fraction of total power (in period range 10.0-500.0 kyr) lying within ±5.0% bands around the 32 L1 integers. Tested whether this fraction's coefficient of variation across windows is unusually low compared to 200 random 32-integer lattices in n ∈ [5, 200].
verdict:
SPLIT FINDING — the 8H lattice is a strict invariant for obliquity and a dominant attractor for eccentricity:

    OBLIQUITY:    plateau 100.0% on L1 → essentially STRICT INVARIANT. Only 0.0% off-lattice content (noise floor). L1 is the complete spectral description of obliquity dynamics.

    ECCENTRICITY: plateau 73.8% on L1 → DOMINANT ATTRACTOR but not strict invariant. 26.2% of power is truly off-lattice. Capture beats 100% of random 32-integer controls (p<1/200).

    INTERPRETATION: For obliquity, there is nothing else — Laskar's dynamics is fully captured by the framework's 8H lattice. For eccentricity, ~26% of power lies in genuinely off-lattice frequencies — either real chaos (Laskar 1989/1994 inner-planet eccentricity diffusion) OR the signature of a conservation law that the framework hasn't yet articulated. The Saturn-anchored structure (Test C-Balance) and the obliquity strict invariance both point at a conservation law specifically for spin-related dynamics; eccentricity chaos may be where the system genuinely wanders.
